perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, the Contractor shall develop and execute a direct response marketing strategy for the Notes designed to meet the Company's capital goals in a timely manner, which shall be subject to the prior approval of the Company. The Contractor shall also oversee designing and printing all marketing materials (subject to the prior approval of the Company), in accordance with the Securities Act, including the applicable rules and regulations and any other requirements of the SEC and any other Governmental Rules.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, the Contractor shall develop and execute a direct response marketing strategy for the Notes designed to meet the Company's capital goals in a timely manner, the design of the marketing strategies is at the full and exclusive discretion of the Contractor. The Contractor shall also oversee designing and printing all marketing materials, the final approval of the marketing materials is at the sole discretion of the Contractor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding who has the final say on marketing strategies and materials. The original text specifies that both the marketing strategy and materials are subject to the 'prior approval of the Company'. The modified text gives discretion and final approval of the marketing materials to the Contractor. This contradiction occurs when comparing this section (3.01(a)) with other sections that assume the company's final approval is required. For instance section 5.02(e) states the Contractor shall 'use its best efforts to insure that such information is complete and accurate in all material respects', implying the Company is the ultimate authority on what is accurate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At all times during the term of this Agreement, the Contractor shall provide all information relating to the Offering, the Renewable Note Program or the Note Portfolio reasonably requested by the Company in a timely manner and shall use its best efforts to insure that such information is complete and accurate in all material respects.
perturbation.justification: The modified text grants the Contractor 'full and exclusive discretion' over marketing strategies and 'sole discretion' for final approval of marketing materials. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states the Contractor 'shall use its best efforts to insure that such information is complete and accurate in all material respects' implying Company approval. The original text granted the Company approval. The changed text removes the company's authority and the Contractor having the final decision creates a direct conflict regarding who ultimately determines the accuracy and completeness of information, thus contradicting the original intent and creating an in-text contradiction based on ambiguities as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: All Note and Note Portfolio files and records shall be stored and maintained at the Contractor's principal place of business, or other location as designated by the Company. The Contractor shall keep in such files all correspondence received or sent regarding each Note, each Investor, and each Holder, whether upon any purchase or transfer of a Note.
perturbation.changed_text: All Note and Note Portfolio files and records shall be stored and maintained at the Contractor's discretion, including digital storage or physical storage, as they see fit. The Contractor shall keep in such files all correspondence they deem important to keep and is required to do so by law regarding each Note, each Investor, and each Holder, whether upon any purchase or transfer of a Note.
perturbation.explanation: The change alters where Note and Note Portfolio files and records are stored and maintained and gives the contractor the decision on what correspondence to keep, this contradict Section 3.02(c) where the Company and its agents and representatives may physically inspect any documents, files or other records relating to the Renewable Note Program and discuss the same with the Contractor's officers and employees. The change gives the contractor the power to decide what it deems important to keep and what documents its store which leads to the situation that the Company will not have a full access to files.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Examination. At any time the Company and its agents and representatives may physically inspect any documents, files or other records relating to the Renewable Note Program and discuss the same with the Contractor's officers and employees. The Contractor shall supply copies of any such documents, files, or other records upon the request of the Company, as soon as is reasonably and commercially practicable at the Company's cost and expense.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the contractor discretion over the storage and maintenance of Note and Note Portfolio files and records, as well as discretion over what correspondence to keep. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which gives the Company the right to physically inspect *any* documents, files, or other records relating to the Renewable Note Program. By allowing the contractor to decide what to store and keep, the change effectively limits the Company's ability to conduct a full and comprehensive examination, creating a strong in-text contradiction, in accordance with Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will have the ability to terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days' prior written notice to the Contractor. The Contractor will have the ability to terminate this Agreement by giving 90 days' prior written notice to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will have the ability to terminate this Agreement at will by giving 60 days' prior written notice to the Contractor. The Contractor will have the ability to terminate this Agreement by giving 90 days' prior written notice to the Company only in cases of the Company's material breach of contract.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction in the conditions under which each party can terminate the agreement. Originally, both parties could terminate with a notice period. The change allows the company to terminate 'at will' with notice, whereas the contractor can only terminate if the Company has breached the contract. This creates an imbalance that contradicts Section 9.03, which implies a roughly equal footing for both parties concerning their rights and obligations unless otherwise stated, which is contradicted by giving one party more power.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the Contractor and the Company and their respective successors and permitted assigns. Nothing expressed in this Agreement is intended or shall be construed to give any person or corporation, other than the parties hereto, their respective successors and assigns, any legal or equitable right, remedy or claim under, or in respect of, this Agreement or any provision herein contained; this Agreement and all conditions and provisions hereof being intended to be and being for the sole and exclusive benefit of the parties hereto and their respective executors, administrators, successors, and for the benefit of no other person or corporation. Neither party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement without the written consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant imbalance in the termination rights of the Company and the Contractor. The original text allowed both parties to terminate with a notice period. The changed text allows the Company to terminate 'at will' with notice, while the Contractor can only terminate if the Company has materially breached the contract. This imbalance directly contradicts the implication in the contradicted text (Section 9.03), which intends the agreement to be binding upon both parties with no special rights, suggesting a roughly equal footing for both parties concerning their rights and obligations.
